recentNews.headline: Apple names John Ternus CEO; Tim Cook to become Executive Chair effective Sept 1, 2026
recentNews.whyItMatters: The most significant leadership transition in Apple's modern history. Hardware chief Ternus (joined 2001, led hardware since 2021) succeeds Cook, who stays on as Executive Chair to oversee strategy and policy. Signals continuity in product/operations focus and ends years of succession speculation; Art Levinson moves to Lead Independent Director.

recentNews.headline: Apple posts record March quarter: $111.2B revenue (+17%), Services all-time record $31B, EPS $2.01
recentNews.whyItMatters: Beat consensus (rev est. ~$108.9B, EPS est. $1.93) on extraordinary iPhone 17 demand and record Services. Gross margin rose to 49.3% from 47.1%. Guided June-quarter revenue up 14-17% YoY, well above expectations, but flagged 'significantly higher' memory costs ahead.

recentNews.headline: Cook: iPhone 17 demand 'off the charts' but supply constraints capped sales
recentNews.whyItMatters: A global memory shortage driven by AI buildouts and tight TSMC advanced-node capacity constrained iPhone 17 supply, meaning Apple left demand on the table. Management warned memory costs will rise materially as suppliers redirect output to AI chips, a key margin watch item for FY26.

recentNews.headline: Google confirms Gemini-powered Siri on track for later in 2026
recentNews.whyItMatters: After repeated Siri/Apple Intelligence delays, Apple's ~$1B/year multiyear deal to run a revamped Siri on Google Gemini (via Apple's Private Cloud Compute) is Apple's answer to its perceived AI lag. Phase 1 ships in iOS 26.5; fuller conversational Siri targeted for iOS 27 this September.

recentNews.headline: Apple picks Google Gemini to power AI Siri; deal to extend beyond Siri into Apple Intelligence
recentNews.whyItMatters: Marks a strategic admission that Apple's in-house models weren't ready. Outsourcing foundational AI to a rival (Google) for ~$1B/year reshapes the AI competitive landscape and raises questions about Apple's long-term model independence and Private Cloud Compute differentiation.

recentNews.headline: Apple overhauls EU App Store fees again to comply with DMA: 5% Core Technology Commission replaces per-install fee
recentNews.whyItMatters: Apple consolidated EU terms into a single model with a 5% Core Technology Commission on digital goods across App Store, web distribution and alternative marketplaces, and dropped fees for free apps. Developers and the Coalition for App Fairness argue it still flouts the DMA; follows a prior EUR500M EU fine, keeping App Store economics a regulatory and revenue risk.

recentNews.headline: Greater China revenue +28% YoY to $20.5B on iPhone 17 strength and subsidies
recentNews.whyItMatters: A sharp turnaround in Apple's most-watched geography after prior softness. iPhone shipments in China rose ~20% in calendar Q1 2026, aided by government subsidies and targeted discounting, easing a key bear-case overhang.

recentNews.headline: Trump tariffs hit Apple's India iPhone plan; India duties raised to 50%, plus 25% threat on non-US iPhones
recentNews.whyItMatters: India is now Apple's #1 iPhone manufacturing country, central to its China-diversification strategy. A 50% US tariff on Indian goods and Trump's threat of a 25% tariff on iPhones not made in the US directly pressure Apple's supply-chain economics, though India assembly reportedly remains cost-competitive.
